text: The patient is placed in the left lateral decubitus position.
A two centimeter incision is made at the seventh intercostal space on the posterior axillary line.
The initial inspection reveals the pleural effusion and, as you may see in this case, 2-3 mm implants are extended on the visceral pleura as well as on the diaphragm and parietal pleura.

After observing a resecable pleural carcinomatosis, the pleural effusion is drained and multiple biopsies are performed to obtain the histology confirmation and start treatment with neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Finally, clearodesis with sterolite talc is made.
That's all and we hope this video has been useful for you.
